items.description: CONCLUSION: ML models show high image-level diagnostic performance for stone detection on NCCT and may support radiologists as decision support tools. items.description: The synthesis of sulfur- and oxygen-bridged cationic [4]-helicenes via a tandem Friedel-Crafts-S(N)Ar reaction of a diaryl sulfide or a diaryl ether with a (thio)salicylic acid has been developed. items.title: Time-dependent risk of falls associated with hypnotic use in hospitalized patients
items.description: CONCLUSIONS: Fall risk associated with hypnotic use is not uniform throughout the day. Short-acting benzodiazepines were linked to evening and nighttime falls, while long-acting benzodiazepines increased morning fall risk. These findings highlighted the need to consider time-specific fall risks when prescribing hypnotics.
items.conclusion: Fall risk associated with hypnotic use is not uniform throughout the day. Short-acting benzodiazepines were linked to evening and nighttime falls, while long-acting benzodiazepines increased morning fall risk
